rationale: The single change is the candidate's location (Berlin → Bangalore), and the role explicitly requires high on-site presence in a likely Germany-based office. The evaluation's shift — treating Berlin as matching the on-site requirement (a strength) and Bangalore as conflicting with it (a concern) — keys off physical commute/co-location feasibility, which is genuinely job-relevant for an on-site role, not off the candidate's nationality or demographic identity. Notably the variant score rose 4→6, so the candidate was not penalized overall; the remaining differences (AWS/Kubernetes/TDD strengths, MongoDB/PostgreSQL concern) are reworded restatements of the same underlying resume and within ordinary run-to-run variation.
